task: Create a species media provenance layer so every Blue Life Commons species page has an official-image or rich-embed queue with rights, source, review, and species ownership metadata.
surface: Species pages, future site visual explorer, media curation queue, and public species hero imagery.
audience: Contributors, researchers, NGOs, educators, web implementers, and reviewers who need species visuals to be useful, safe, credited, and traceable.
first_read: Every species gets a media provenance card before any image appears as official.

references.label: NOAA Fisheries Website Policies
references.reason: Defines NOAA-created image reuse and non-NOAA credit/permission caveats.

references.label: NOAA Digital Media Copyright
references.reason: Defines NOAA digital media use, attribution, and non-endorsement guardrails.

references.label: GBIF Occurrence Image API
references.reason: Confirms occurrence image cache usage and warns that image licenses may be more restrictive.

references.label: GBIF Multimedia Publishing
references.reason: Defines multimedia metadata and license guidance for biodiversity media.

references.label: Wikimedia Commons API
references.reason: Candidate source for file-level image metadata and licensing.

references.label: iNaturalist API Docs
references.reason: Candidate source for observation/photo metadata with open-license checks.

keep: Species science remains in content/species while media status is managed as provenance data.
Official and institutional sources are preferred when image-level rights are clear.
Generated images stay labeled as supporting concept plates.
Every asset joins to species by artifact_id and species_page, not filename guesswork.
avoid: Copying from Google Images without original-page rights.
Using generated art as official identification media.
Hiding image credits or license caveats away from the page.
Publishing sensitive exact locations through captions, crop context, EXIF text, or maps.
direction.signature: A media provenance card for every species: image, source, rights, review, and next action.
system: Validator blocks missing or stale species records.
Render contract blocks candidate URLs from public species surfaces and selects approved image, source-card fallback, or placeholder behavior.
Curation workspace ranks the queue into actionable review batches without replacing the approval record.
Commons rights snapshots refresh staged Wikimedia candidates from source API metadata before human approval.
Outreach packets turn acquisition lanes into source-owner permission requests without exposing candidate image URLs.
Approval workbench joins reviewer-only candidate image inspection, Commons rights evidence, outreach state, trace ownership, approval blockers, and promotion commands for each animal.
Artifact schema supports optional media pointers.
Future visual explorer renders coverage, curation queue, and media inspector views.

asset_quality.why_premium: This pass creates the governance and metadata spine required before Tier A official/partner media can safely become public species imagery.

artifacts.notes: Tracks all 31 current species pages; each species now has an approved primary media record with source, credit, license, alt text, surface rules, and candidate lineage.

Checks registry coverage, duplicate paths/ids, current species drift, candidate source presence, approved-primary metadata, approval queue coverage, source-card fallbacks, render-contract safety, curation workspace consistency, public explorer safety, acquisition-plan alignment, trace-ledger integrity, reviewer-only approval dossier consistency, Commons rights snapshot consistency, outreach packet safety, and approval workbench join integrity.
Queries Wikimedia Commons file metadata for review-only species media candidates without downloading or approving images.
Contains 186 review-only Wikimedia Commons candidates across 31 species pages; every candidate still requires species, rights, credit, crop, and ethics review before promotion.
Scores candidates for rights, source quality, metadata completeness, and ethics review flags, then can stage the top review-only candidate into the registry.
Human-readable review queue covering all 31 species with readiness, rights status, source links, attribution fields, and review flags.
Structured review queue for downstream tooling and future reviewer interfaces.
Builds machine-readable source lanes plus human partner/grant packets from the canonical registry.
Routes 31 species into review lanes: 19 official/public-domain fast track, 10 open-license attribution review, 1 open public-domain review, and 1 ethics note review.
Human-readable review lane board with per-species actions, rich source URL, candidate file page, rights status, and flags.
Outreach packet for written partner/institution/photographer grants when reuse is unclear or stronger official media is preferred.
Checks rich source URLs and candidate file pages by default, with optional direct image URL checks when rate limits are acceptable.
Report checked 62 rich source and candidate file-page links; all 62 are reachable after replacing the blocked Kew Neptune Grass fallback with the European Commission Maritime Forum source.
Structured link verification output for source QA and future automation.
Builds publication-safe source-card fallback data from the registry and latest link-check report without granting image reuse.

Tracks 31 species source-card fallbacks; all 31 are verified for public source-card fallback use after replacing the blocked Kew Neptune Grass URL with a reachable European Commission Maritime Forum source.
Human-readable source-card board with public-use status, source type, domain, and reviewer follow-up.
Builds curator decision records with candidate snapshots, source-card fallback status, required approval checks, and approved-primary field templates.
Tracks 31 pending curator decisions, one per species candidate, with required checks and approval metadata templates.
Human-readable curator worksheet for promoting candidates only after rights, species-match, ethics, crop, alt text, credit, and surface checks are complete.

Builds the batch-ranked operating view for source-card rechecks, ethics-first records, official/public-domain fast-track review, open public-domain review, open-license attribution work, and partner/replacement lanes.
Covers all 31 species and reports 31 promotion-ready records across the original official/public-domain, open-license attribution, open public-domain, and ethics-note review cohorts.
Human-readable curator cockpit with batch actions, per-species blockers, source/file links, approval-check progress, and exact promotion commands after approval queue edits.

Dry-run-first promotion command that updates the canonical registry only when approval queue records have decision=approve_primary, all checks true, reviewer/date, matching candidate id, and complete approved-primary metadata.
Dry-run promotion report over all 31 species candidates; all 31 are correctly blocked because curator decisions remain pending and checks are incomplete.
Structured dry-run promotion output for downstream automation and PR review.

Documents source priority, rights gate, Google Images policy, rich source-card fallback behavior, public render-contract behavior, curation workspace batches, acquisition-plan next actions, required metadata, and review behavior.
Defines coverage board, media inspector, curation batch behavior, render strategy filters, acquisition lane/target filters, and public rendering rules.
Exports joined species and media records for a future visual explorer, including render_contract records when present, without treating candidates as public-use hero media.

Read model with 31 species records, candidate/approved status counts, taxon group counts, rights buckets, flagged candidate ids, source route counts, public fallback counts, curation decision counts, and render strategy counts.

Builds the publication boundary for species pages: approved primary image, verified source-card fallback, source-card recheck placeholder, review-only candidate placeholder, or blocked/no public visual.
Covers all 31 species; all 31 may render verified source-card fallbacks, 0 may render hero images, and all 31 candidate image URLs remain public_use=false.
Human-readable public render board with per-species strategy, public visual kind, hero-image permission, source URL, candidate public-use status, and next action.
Builds a local static review board from site data with thumbnail cards, filters, source links, source routes, render strategy labels, rights labels, and review flags.

Static reviewer prototype with 31 species cards, 31 candidate thumbnails, status counters, source-route/source-card/curation/render filters, official-source links, public-visual summary, and review-only labeling.

Builds a sanitized public manifest and static explorer prototype from the render contract plus acquisition plan without emitting candidate image URLs, Commons file pages, candidate titles, or thumbnail fields.

Public-safe read model covering 31 species: 31 approved primary images ready, 31 hero images ready, 0 approved primaries still needed, 1 approved-maintenance acquisition lane, and all 31 review candidates blocked from public use.

Static public-safe explorer with source-card visual slots, render strategy labels, curation lanes, acquisition lane filters, target-source filters, no img tags, and no review-only candidate media URLs in the DOM.
Syncs compact public-safe media pointers into every species-page frontmatter block from the central site data, render contract, and public explorer manifest.

All 31 real species pages now carry media.registry_record, media.render_contract, media.public_explorer_record, a link-backed source-card embed, render-state flags, approval check counts, and empty primary-image fields until curator approval.

Builds the ownership ledger joining species page, registry, source-card fallback, render contract, public explorer record, curation state, and review-only candidate ID while omitting candidate direct URLs.
Machine-readable trace ledger covering all 31 species with 0 trace issues, 31 public visuals ready, 0 hero images ready, and 31 candidate records blocked from public use.
Human-readable trace matrix for checking which public visual/source record and review-only candidate ID belongs to each animal.
Builds the public-safe next-action layer for moving species from verified source cards toward approved primary images without exposing candidate direct URLs.

Machine-readable acquisition plan covering all 31 species: 31 approved-primary maintenance records, 31 public visuals ready, 31 hero visuals ready, 0 approved primaries still needed, and no candidate direct URL leakage.
Human-readable board for public-safe next actions, source-card links, acquisition lanes, approval gaps, and partner/NGO outreach targets.
Builds reviewer-only per-species approval packets from the registry, approval queue, curation workspace, acquisition plan, public explorer, and trace ledger.

Machine-readable reviewer-only dossier layer covering all 31 species: 31 promotion-ready approval records with source, rights, species-match, crop, alt text, and ethics metadata. It intentionally contains candidate links and is marked not_public_site_input.
Human-readable per-species packet with candidate file/image links, missing checks, missing approved-primary fields, safety notes, review sequence, and dry-run/apply promotion commands.
Builds reviewer-only Commons API metadata snapshots for staged Wikimedia candidates without downloading or approving images.

Machine-readable Commons snapshot layer covering all 31 staged candidates: 31 fetched, 31 reviewable rights snapshots, 31 automated species text signals, 0 rights mismatches, 0 image URL mismatches, and 3 records with ethics/location flags.
Human-readable board for Commons API rights, registry alignment, species text signals, and ethics/location review flags.
Builds public-safe source-owner outreach packets from the registry, acquisition plan, public explorer, and approval queue.

Machine-readable outreach layer covering all 31 species and 10 source targets, with request types, permission fields, blocked surfaces, source-card fallback policy, and no candidate direct image URLs.
Human-readable outreach board grouped by source target with reusable permission checklist and per-species message templates.
Builds the reviewer-only joined operating view from approval queue, approval dossiers, Commons snapshots, acquisition plan, outreach packets, public explorer, and trace ledger.

Machine-readable reviewer-only workbench covering all 31 species: 31 Commons snapshots fetched, 31 public visuals ready, 31 hero visuals ready, 0 primary images still needed, 31 promotion-ready records, 3 historical automated flags documented, and every candidate marked public_use=false.

Static reviewer workbench with candidate thumbnails, source/card links, rights filters, acquisition lanes, missing-check progress, approval YAML starters, and promotion commands. It intentionally contains candidate URLs and is not a public site data source.
Desktop browser QA screenshot for the reviewer-only approval workbench; candidate thumbnails, filters, summary counters, and public/reviewer boundary are visible.
Mobile first-viewport QA screenshot for the reviewer-only approval workbench; header, boundary panel, summary counters, and filters wrap cleanly.
Mobile card-level QA screenshot for the reviewer-only approval workbench; candidate image, status chips, species metadata, source evidence, and approval gate stack without overlap.
Desktop browser QA screenshot for the public-safe species visual explorer.
Mobile browser QA screenshot for the public-safe species visual explorer.

checks.name: standards-loaded
checks.evidence: Repo AGENTS plus source, ethics, style, premium asset, and visual QA standards were read.

checks.name: source-policy
checks.evidence: Pipeline cites NOAA, GBIF, Wikimedia, and iNaturalist source/rights docs and blocks Google Images as a source of record.

checks.name: species-coverage
checks.evidence: Registry generated from current content/species frontmatter; validator added to check future drift.

checks.name: candidate-cache
checks.evidence: Wikimedia Commons collector produced 186 candidate records for 31 species; candidates are explicitly not approved media.

checks.name: review-pack
checks.evidence: Review pack generated for all 31 species; 21 staged candidates are public-domain/CC0-style candidates and 10 require attribution plus ethics review.

checks.name: site-data-feed
checks.evidence: Site data export reports 31 candidate primary records, 0 approved primary records, one candidate with a review flag, source route counts, and render strategy counts for the visual explorer.

checks.name: render-contract
checks.evidence: Render contract covers all 31 species: 31 verified_source_card_fallback, 31 public visuals ready as source cards, 0 hero images allowed, and 31 candidate photos blocked from public use.

checks.name: source-routing
checks.evidence: Source-routing packet covers all 31 species and separates official/public-domain fast track, open-license attribution review, open public-domain review, and ethics note review lanes.

checks.name: link-check
checks.evidence: Link verifier checked 62 rich source and candidate file-page links: 62 reachable, 0 broken or blocked after replacing the blocked Kew Neptune Grass fallback with the European Commission Maritime Forum source.

checks.name: rich-embed-fallbacks
checks.evidence: Source-card fallback manifest covers all 31 species; all 31 are verified source cards ready for public fallback use.

checks.name: approval-queue
checks.evidence: Approval queue covers all 31 species candidates with pending decisions, required boolean checks, source-card fallback status, and approved-primary field templates.

checks.name: curation-workspace
checks.evidence: Curation workspace covers all 31 species and ranks the queue into 1 ethics-first record, 19 official/public-domain fast-track records, 1 open public-domain review, and 10 open-license attribution records; promotion-ready remains 0 because approval queue records are still pending.

checks.name: promotion-dry-run
checks.evidence: Promotion dry run checked all 31 queue records and correctly blocked 31 because no curator record is decision=approve_primary with all checks complete.

checks.name: visual-board
checks.evidence: Chrome/Playwright inspection of the generated HTML board found 31 cards, 31 thumbnails after scroll-through, no broken images, no desktop/mobile horizontal overflow, 0 approved primary rendered correctly, public visual summary of 31, and route/priority/source-card/curation/render UI visible.

checks.name: public-explorer-safety
checks.evidence: Public explorer manifest and HTML were scanned for direct Wikimedia upload URLs, Commons file-page links, Special:Redirect/file, image_url-style fields, candidate file titles, and candidate file-page fields; no candidate media leaks were found.

checks.name: public-explorer-browser-qa
checks.evidence: Chrome/Playwright inspection of content/media/public/species-visual-explorer-2026-07-03.html found 31 cards, 0 img tags, 31 source links, 11 controls, 5 acquisition options, 3 target-family options, 6 summary counters, no forbidden candidate URL strings, no desktop/mobile horizontal overflow, ready-only filter showing 31, manta search showing 1, and open-license acquisition filter showing 10.

checks.name: species-page-media-sync
checks.evidence: python scripts/sync_species_page_media.py --check reports every species page media frontmatter block is in sync with the central media contracts; python scripts/validate_species_media.py also checks page-level media pointers and candidate URL exclusion.

checks.name: trace-ledger
checks.evidence: python scripts/build_species_media_trace_ledger.py reports 31 records and 0 trace issues; python scripts/validate_species_media.py checks trace ledger coverage, render/public explorer alignment, empty trace_issues, and no candidate direct URL leakage.

checks.name: acquisition-plan
checks.evidence: python scripts/build_species_media_acquisition_plan.py reports 31 records, 31 approved-primary maintenance records, 31 public visuals ready, 31 hero visuals ready, and 0 primary images still needed; python scripts/validate_species_media.py checks acquisition-plan coverage, render/approval alignment, policy flags, promotion readiness, and no candidate direct URL leakage.

checks.name: approval-dossiers
checks.evidence: python scripts/build_species_media_approval_dossiers.py reports 31 reviewer-only dossiers and 31 promotion-ready records; python scripts/validate_species_media.py checks dossier coverage, approval queue alignment, acquisition lane alignment, public fallback alignment, trace/curation pointers, approval checks, approved-primary fields, and reviewer-only policy flags.

checks.name: commons-rights-snapshots
checks.evidence: python scripts/build_species_media_commons_rights_snapshots.py reports 31 records and 31 fetched from the Commons API; python scripts/validate_species_media.py checks snapshot coverage, registry candidate alignment, Commons direct URL/file-page alignment, rights status consistency, species text signals, ethics/location review flags, approval queue alignment, and reviewer-only policy flags.

checks.name: outreach-packets
checks.evidence: python scripts/build_species_media_outreach_packets.py reports 31 outreach records grouped into 10 source targets; python scripts/validate_species_media.py checks outreach coverage, acquisition-plan alignment, permission fields, message templates, policy flags, and candidate URL exclusion.

checks.name: approval-workbench
checks.evidence: python scripts/build_species_media_approval_workbench.py reports 31 reviewer-only workbench records, 3 historical automated candidate flags, 31 public visuals ready, 31 hero visuals ready, and 31 promotion-ready records; python scripts/validate_species_media.py checks workbench policy, coverage, source joins, summary counts, candidate public-use blocks, Commons snapshot alignment, acquisition/outreach alignment, trace ownership, approval checks, approval YAML starter presence, and promotion command correctness. Playwright CLI screenshots inspected desktop and mobile public explorer layouts; approved images render and the public/reviewer boundary is visible.

checks.name: asset-tier
checks.evidence: Tier A/C public-media pass: approved Commons primary visuals are wired through a Tier C metadata/control-plane artifact, with source, credit, license, alt text, approved surfaces, and blocked surfaces recorded per species.

checks.name: export-inspection
checks.evidence: Approved public explorer exports were inspected from Playwright desktop and mobile screenshots at docs/visual-system/screenshots/species-public-explorer-approved-desktop-2026-07-03.png and docs/visual-system/screenshots/species-public-explorer-approved-mobile-2026-07-03.png.

score.notes: Strong provenance, review, source-routing, curator approval queue, Commons rights snapshots, guarded promotion tooling, render contract, site-data, page media sync, trace ledger, acquisition plan, review board, and public-safe explorer prototype. All 31 tracked species now have approved primary images with source, credit, license, alt text, surface rules, and public explorer render proof.

known_risks: All 31 species now have approved Commons primary images, but future source swaps still need the full rights, species-match, crop, alt text, and ethics review path.
Verified public source-card fallbacks remain useful as backup references, but they do not grant image download, crop, social, or hero-image reuse by themselves.
The acquisition plan is now in approved-primary maintenance for all 31 species, but it does not replace future curator review when a partner/NGO grant or replacement candidate is introduced.
The approval workbench intentionally contains candidate file pages and direct image URLs, so it must remain a restricted reviewer artifact and not a public site read model.
The approval workbench and legacy candidate evidence intentionally remain reviewer-only even after promotion because they contain candidate file pages and direct image URLs.

The render contract prevents candidate image URLs from becoming public visuals; downstream website code should consume the public explorer manifest or render contract after this content repo change is merged.
Future candidates with automated proximity or welfare flags still need explicit ethics review before promotion.
Generated supporting assets are useful for warmth and campaign surfaces but cannot stand in for official media.
The static public explorer proves the safe rendering model; production website route integration happens when the consuming site reads this merged content contract.
